programme.title: Weepy French Onion Soup - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Weepy French Onion Soup. But the four weepy onions they need are VERY tricky to find. Will Humble, Grumble, and Stumble be able to spot all the onions and get back to the kitchen without crying too much? Watch and see!

programme.title: Put On Your Shoes - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a Super Simple Song all about getting dressed! Sing along as we put on our shoes, jacket, hat, and scarf and then head out for a walk with the family.

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: I Like To Ride My Bicycle (Mr. Monkey) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Let's go on a bike ride with Mr. Monkey and friends as we sing along to this Mr. Monkey version of the original Super Simple Song, "I Like To Ride My Bicycle." This is a great song to introduce during transportation themes! I like to ride my bicycle. I ride it every day. I ride it when I go to school. Or when I want to play.

programme.title: What's Your Name? - Kids Song - Finny the Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny the Shark is saying "Hello" and asking the question "What's your name?" This is a fun song to start the day at preschool! Hello, hello. What’s your name? Hello, hello. What’s your name? My name is Finny! My name is Myrtle! Nice to meet you.

programme.title: Wind The Bobbin Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Wind the bobbin up. Wind the bobbin up. Pull, pull, clap, clap, clap.” Get up and dance with this super fun version of the traditional English nursery rhyme.

programme.title: Hot Cross Buns - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s one of our favorite traditional nursery rhymes about food, made super simple. Come sing along with the Chef from our “Do You Know The Muffin Man?” and “Pat-A-Cake” videos. Yum yum!

programme.title: Flying Dragon Fruit Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Come forth brave warriors and watch the Bumble Nums catch some Flying Dragon Fruits for their fiery Flying Dragon Fruit Cake! But be careful! They breathe fire and are very fast!

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Maggie wants to be a buddy-cop-duo with her pet Chihuahua, Kiki, and help people in need. So, Jett delivers police costumes and joins them in patrolling the town. Jett flies to Hamburg to deliver a pair of mini vacuum cleaners to help Lars and his Dad clean up a spillage of popcorn across their impressive model village. They have trouble reaching popcorn inside the village's train tunnel.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Peiru and her dad are hiking the Great Wall of China in Beijing. Heavy snowfall has made the Great Wall difficult to traverse, so Peiru orders winter hiking gear. Dale wants to be in an underwater bike race with his dad, and help him win. So Jett delivers a tandem bicycle and agrees to join their team. Things take a turn when Golden Boy enters the race and sends them off-course.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A toddler in South Korea named Ha-Jun has a cold but doesn't like the taste of the medicine that his Dad needs to give him. Ha-Jun's sister Yuna calls the Super Wings for help. A boy named Geir in Iceland goes with his Mom on a tour to see arctic foxes in their natural habitat. Geir orders an arctic fox costume to wear so the shy foxes will come out of their caves and meet him.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers a huge inflatable sword to Neele, an accessory for the balloon version of the legendary dragon-fighter Siegfried she is flying with her Papa in the annual balloon festival. Fabio loves soccer but he is not interested in history. At the San Augustin Archeological Park, he accidentally drops his ball into one of the sites with many ancient statues! Jett delivers him a stretchy claw.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a girl named Alani notices that the sea level is rising around the island of Tuvalu she orders a huge lifeboat for all the people in her village to ride on. Jett finds himself stuck in ice in Antarctica courtesy of Golden Boy, Golden Wheels, and a giant Golden Robot. He is rescued by World Aircraft and learns that Golden Boy has caused lots of global warming problems around the planet.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A passenger airplane bound for Italy has to make an emergency landing on World Aircraft. When Lime offers to prepare a meal for the passengers, Mano, a young boy passenger, makes a popular suggestion: pizza! Ratu and his Dad are excited about a "survival challenge" on one of Fiji's Desert Islands, but they forgot to bring their survival guide to teach them how to filter water!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers flowery mooncakes to Meilin, a young girl who wants to celebrate the Mid-Autumn Festival by leaving a gift for the Moon Lady, who is said to descend from the moon according to Chinese folklore. A girl named Emma lives in Transinne, Belgium and she asks the Super Wings if they can help her with her solar system school project.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Astra and Rover continue to help Emma, her Dad, and spaceship Rick see the solar system. Trouble is Golden Boy and Golden Wheels tag along! Jett delivers a claw grabber to Marcia, a little girl playing a treasure hunt game in which she searches for hidden treasure notes in the Rio Abiseo National Park in Peru. Our Super Wing helps her in her search!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets encounter three pandas on a country road in Chengdu, China. They try to help get the pandas off the highway and back to the panda centre. A girl asks the Super Wings for help in locating a dolphin in the waters off Iceland. Jett and Paul deliver a sonar device that can track dolphin whistles.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a princess's dogs need to attend training school she worries that they're too playful to pass. Jett and Donnie deliver some special training suits to help them succeed. When the Super Pets accidentally knock down a birthday piñata at a house outside of Mexico City, Jett and Jerome deliver a huge piñata replacement for the boy's party.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets visit Pisa, Italy where some street cats have made a mess spilling recycling bins. Jett and a new Super Wing called Shine deliver tongs and help with the clean up. The Super Pets are in Pohang, South Korea where they meet a girl whose robot is in need of repairs. Jett and Jerome deliver a much needed repair kit and some robot dance lessons!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets meet a boy in the Serengeti National Park whose ball rolls into an ostrich nest. The mother ostrich thinks the ball is one of her eggs and won't give it back. When a hippo gets stuck on a waterslide at a community swimming pool in Kenya, a boy calls on the Super Wings to deliver a hippo-sized life ring to help free it.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers winter clothes to a little girl living in Egypt's Sahara Desert where a cold snap has left the desert snowy. Jett and Astra show her how to enjoy the snow. After the Super Pets accidentally knock down some Turkish rugs in an Istanbul market, Jett and Ellie deliver a magic lamp that can summon two genies.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A boy, Kima, and his Dad are trekking through the Himalaya mountains in Nepal. The Super Wings bring an LED torch to help them. Trouble strikes when a baby yeti startles them. Vo, the Mars rover, is celebrating his birthday but misses his friend Catalina. Jett and Astra come to the rescue and promise to bring Catalina and her ginormous gift to Mars.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: For Children's Day, Wang Min's Dad has invited his son and his classmates to visit his toy factory. While cleaning up before the visit, a robot toy goes on a rampage. The Super Pets encounter an itchy lion at a sanctuary in South Africa. Jett and Astra deliver a back scratcher, but the lion's itchiness is stronger than they realized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets go into a video game arcade and play a video game called Dessert Kingdom. They are watched by a boy named Yoonho and they decide to go into the game itself. When the Super Pets unearth a woolly mammoth, a Nenets boy wants to keep it. The mammoth must be able to pull a sleigh so Jett and Dizzy deliver a mammoth size sleigh.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a T-Rex skeleton on display in a Copenhagen museum is brought to life, a boy orders a remote controller to summon a giant robot T-Rex that he built to help calm it. Jett and the others arrive in the Jurassic Period to return Tyranno to her proper place in time. A volcano erupts throwing fireballs everywhere and causing a dinosaur stampede.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets spend a day at a Pet Hotel in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The daughter of the hotel owner gets help from the Super Wings to keep them well behaved. After the Jet Pets' Mini Base collides with a small comet, Jett and Astra rush a space scanner to a young boy and together and the three search space for the small 'Baba' comet.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Baby's innate curiosity about all they see and hear increases on a daily basis. Before long, they begin to associate words with objects and to imitate and articulate sounds. Why not give their eyes and ears something wonderful to feast upon? Baby Shakespeare presents little ones with 12 common words that can be found within the rich context of classic poems and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Baby Lullaby takes your baby on a soothing journey with classical lullabies by Bach, Brahms and Mozart. Enjoy relaxing ocean waves, sweet puppet shows and tranquil images. From fun time to rest time, Baby Lullaby is the perfect way to spend special time with your baby.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Dive in with your little explorer across the seas to meet amazing animals living in the ocean, coral reef, and polar regions. Overflowing with underwater footage of whales, dolphins, tropical fish, and more, Neptune's Oceans invites you and your baby on an oceanic voyage of discovery!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Take your little explorer on an exotic musical expedition to meet wild animals from across the globe. Journey to the jungle, savannah, desert and forest.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies develop, their understanding of the world, and how its pieces "fit" together, continues to progress. DISCOVERING SHAPES playfully introduces five basic forms circle, oval, triangle, square and rectangle as each appears in the context of toys, nature and everyday objects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies grow and change, so does the landscape around them. From fiery red leaves to falling snowflakes; from flowers in bloom to fireworks in July, every season of the year has its own special magic just waiting to be discovered.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: The Sandbox
programme.desc: The Sandbox is where our favorite Baby Einstein characters gather to play, problem-solve and have fun. Each five-minute episode explores an area of curiosity relevant to our 2-4 year old audience. Since the four weathered planks of wood that make up the structure of their sandbox are no obstacles for their imaginations, the entire universe is available to them for each new adventure.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Perhaps the sweetest sounds you'll ever hear are the babbles and coos that one day become ma-ma, da-da and I love you. Besides being music to your ears, these baby steps of language change everything as your little one discovers an exciting, powerful new way of relating to the world! Baby's First Sounds invites both of you to share these memorable moments: those precious ''firsts'' that come around once in your baby's lifetime.

programme.title: Wheels on the Bus & More Nursery Rhymes - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Welcome to the mystical and of the Little Treehouse! where you are take non magical nursery rhymes journey. Kids will laugh, dance, sing, and play along with our video, learning kids songs like I spy song, to market to market, animals sounds song, mary had a little lamb, row row row your boat and much, much more while enjoying our friendly characters.

programme.title: Wheels On The Garbage Truck + More Nursery Rhymes - Little Treehouse
programme.desc: Hey Kids, Little Treehouse is back with more nursery rhymes for toddlers that you will enjoy. Wheels on the Garbage Truck is here to take you on a fun ride where we sing-along, dance and have fun.Come join in this preschool adventure and enjoy it with your friends. Little Treehouse has some amazing animated learning videos and more baby rhymes collection like,Who Stole My Toy, Happy Elephant, Chew Your Food, Doctor Checkup Song, Swimming Song, Peek A Boo, Rock a Bye Baby and many more Kindergarten Songs.

programme.title: Farmees Movie Compilation
programme.desc: Farmees brings you the best of nursery rhymes and kids songs. Your kids are sure to enjoy!

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids
programme.desc: Welcome to #Dinobees, a funny show for kids. It revolves around a family that discovers and adopts three dinosaurs. This comedy cartoon is perfect entertainment for toddlers.Dinobees has some amazing 3d animations. Get ready to have great jungle adventures with cartoon animals and dinosaurs.This entertaining playlist is a collection of videos for children like Fire Discovery, Fruits of Labour, Magical Berries, Catch me If You can, Glue Trap, Mis Splits, I Wanna Fly, The Gophers, Baby Sitter, Frozen Lake, Bubble Trouble, Where's My Water and many more videos.

programme.title: Super Supremes Dinosaur Song & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: The kids are so excited to learn about dinosaurs. Here is a collection of our best videos.

programme.title: Jingle Bells and Christmas Songs - Little Eddie
programme.desc: Feel the Christmas holiday spirit with Little Eddie and his favorite Xmas songs like Jingle bells, we wish you a Merry Christmas, 12 days of Christmas, Deck the halls, joy to the world, silent nights and more Christmas carols & music for kids and babies.

programme.title: Super Car Royce - Back To Back
programme.desc: Watch this compilation from Super Car Royce Back To School!

programme.title: Little Red Car - Popular Songs
programme.desc: Watch this whole bunch of popular songs by the Little Red Car for kids. Hope you enjoy them!

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik decides to cripple Mobius' three major transit systems: Rail, Air, and Sea in order to fully gain control of the planet. Sonic has to circumnavigate the planet in order to save the day.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik invents a robot coach -- the "Coachnik", to put S and G through a "Training Camp" to improve their skills. Then Coachnik puts a bomb in Grounder's head as a booby trap for Sonic, and a football game ensues with Grounder's head as the ball.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Sonic and Tails help out an Owl/Chilidog Diner owner, named Bert Whoo, when Robotnik uses his henchbots to try to collect protection money. When the henchbots prove to be no match for Sonic.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Coconuts inadvertantly creates a "super power" formula, when his janitor bucket spills in the lab. Robotnik falls into it and becomes "Super Robotnik", then challenges Sonic to a fight, with all of Mobius as the stakes. With Prof. Von...

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When outlaws chase a sheriff out of a western town, Sonic takes over. But the outlaws are robots built by Robotnik. And when they capture a pretty possum of a saloon girl, Sonic meets Robotnik for a showdown.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic reluctantly takes Tails with him to the Big City to find some cotter pins for the Freedom Fighters catapults. While in the City, Sonic is nearly eaten alive by his old dog Muttski, now a roboti-sized guard dog in the Junk Heaps

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters, with new addition, Dulcy, develop a De-Roboti-sizer and try it out on Bunnie. It's a success and, against Sally's wishes, Sonic battles Robotnik's newest weapon, a Virtual Reality Shriek-Bot.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters run into a Swashbuckler, named Ari, battling Swat-Bots. Sonic diverts them, allowing Ari to escape. After hearing that Robotnik is holding his band of Freedom Fighters hostage, Ari decides to help Sonic free them.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic attempts a solo rescue to prevent prisoners from being roboti-sized and is hit by a Memory Scrambler that erases short-term memory.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After a disastrous failed mission, Sonic laments that they can't go back and prevent Robotnik's take-over. Dulcy tells them they can and takes Sonic and Sally to the Floating Island where they find the "Time Stones".

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Antoine decides to study martial arts but after he wrecks most of Knothole Sonic sends him off to practice in the woods. //Tails begins his training to become a real Freedom Fighter by learning tracking skills while on a camping trip.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic and Tails befriend a pair of aliens who crash land their spaceship on Mobius. Sonic has to avoid Robotnik's ULTRA-GRAVITIZER (sort of a hedgehog flypaper) to help fix their ship and blast-off back home.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic loses his memory when Robotnik uses a hypnotic flower pollen on him, and tells him he's just an "ordinary" guy.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Outraged by a new hit song written about Sonic, Robotnik outlaws music on Mobius then takes pop star, Catty Carlisle captive. With his MD (Music Destroyer) Robot, Robotnik terrorizes the music loving Mobius population till Sonic rescues Catty.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sonic The Hedgehog
programme.desc: Coconuts is exiled by Robotnik to a remote jungle, where he uses a giant baby ape to destroy villages. But Sonic exposes Coconuts as a robot, and the baby ape's real Dad takes Coconuts and the baby ape away for a spanking.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: In Rainbow Village, people are busy preparing for a dance marathon, a competition in which all of the villagers participate. Ling Ling is very sad that Ellie decided not to participate. Ellie is not a confident dancer so she doesn't want to participate. // Rainbow Ruby's friends tell her that they are now friends with Felicia. They were playing hide and seek but Felicia is such a good hider that she's refused to come out even though she's won the game.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries. Kiki accidentally picked magical Plumen berries instead of the delicious rainbow berries she was trying to gather.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ellie's and Jessy's hair are a complete mess after Felicia accidentally causes some Rainbow fruit to fall on them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a hairdresser. // It's winter in Rainbow Village. Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that last night a huge ice mountain appeared so everyone is enjoying an ice sculpture festival. But Jessy seems to have a toothache.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The ground is all wet in Rainbow Village. What's going on? The friends tell Ruby that Jessy's tears have soaked the ground. Jessy accidentally ate Ellie's magic beans and became as huge as a giant. // Thunderbell is very sad because his best friend, Mr. Sloth, is angry at him. Rainbow Ruby thinks up the best way to apologize to Mr. Sloth, who loves performances. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a choreographer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell, Ellie, and Kiki are looking hard for something. It turns out that when Thunderbell was trying on Kiki's crown, he accidentally dropped it and a gem went missing. Kiki sadly says that a princess' crown is worthless without a jewel. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that this year they won't be able to enjoy the traditional rainbow dumplings as the recipe was accidentally torn to pieces. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a chef.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a gardener.// Ling Ling is setting up a 'Thankfulness' tree at the center of the town square. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are on a train and they are concerned that the train won't stop. The engine is even smoking! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a firefighter. // Paige is extremely upset. It turns out that she's been preparing an awesome fashion show but now it had to be cancelled because Gina accidentally spilled a bunch of fruit onto the stage.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A Flower Festival is underway in Rainbow Village and Ling Ling has called Rainbow Ruby to help her judge the "best flower" contest. But just as Rainbow Ruby starts looking around at the flowers, Kiki's pet dog Pom Pom starts acting unusual and running around frantically, destroying the festival! // Gina and Jessy are with a huge fish that's injured by the lake. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Marine Biologist. Rainbow Ruby uses her encyclopedia to find out that the fish is actually a dolphin.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Everyone is frightened because some visiting birds keep trying to sit on their heads. According to Chirpee, the birds just enjoy sitting on people's heads. Rainbow Ruby designs hats where the birds can comfortably sit without disturbing the villagers. // The village friends were planning to have some fun with the rainbow fish but they're all disappointed to discover that the lake has frozen over.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The friends are very tired because Mr. Moon hasn't come to sing them their nightly lullaby. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an Astronaut and flies to space to look for Mr. Moon with her friends. // In Rainbow Village, a super frantic Thunderbell is high up in the air, trapped inside a giant bubble. Thunderbell got trapped in a giant bubble in the middle of a bubble bath. In order to find out a way to bring Thunderbell back to earth, Rainbow Ruby turns into a scientist.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an architect and builds a house that suits Felicia's demands. // Gina is extremely worried. She needs strawberries for the Rainbow Fruit Ice Cream Festival that's starting soon but the strawberries are all mixed in with other red objects.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a farmer. // Thunderbell has a terrible tummy ache. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Microbiologist to find out why Thunderbell is sick. Rainbow Ruby uses a microscope to find a bacterium inside the cheesecake Thunderbell had but Felicia accidentally casts a spell that makes the bacterium gigantic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: When Ruby and Choco arrive at the village, they find a very worried looking Ling Ling. Gina, Ellie, and Thunderbell went camping in Rainbow Forest but they saw a monster in the forest, got scared and had to run away. // Ruby is struggling to make a doll with her dad. Ruby and Choco get their suitcase and as Ruby swings her umbrella, there they are, in front of Rainbow fountain. Mayor Ling Ling tells her that today is the day of Princess Kiki's ballet performance.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's the first day of spring in Strawberryland, at least it's supposed to be, but with the cold winds blowing and frost on the ground it looks as though spring isn't going to come this year.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: When Huckleberry and Blueberry's Patti Persimmon-style mystery story is rejected by an online mystery magazine, Blueberry and Huck have to come up with a new way to raise support for Huckleberry's Hotel for Homeless Pets back in Berry Big City. Strawberry suggests they hold a big dog show! Plum makes over-ambitious plans to display talents that her dog does not quite have, trying to teach him complicated dance routines.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: The girls plan a formal afternoon lawn party tea to inaugurate their new dog park. Plum is in charge of decorations, Cherry music, and Strawberry snacks. There's even a giant, dog-shaped ice sculpture. As the girls get dressed, Raspberry puts the finishing touches on the high-fashion hats that will accompany their beautiful gowns. At last everything is ready. But when the gate opens... the puppies back away!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake's friends watch her with wide eyes as she everrrrr-so-gently squirts a layer of blueberry smoothie into a tall glass, trying to not disturb the five other multi-colored layers beneath it. It's her tenth attempt to create the world's first Rainbow Smoothie! She has even painted one wall of her café rainbow colors in honor of the soon-to-be new item on the menu. No one dares breathe for fear of spoiling the delicate touch of a master smoothie-squirter at work.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: It's a busy day at Lemon's salon, a charming place that is more than just a hair salon; it's one of favorite places for Strawberry and her friends to relax and socialize. All the girls are getting their hair done for the gala grand opening of Mr. Longface's new lawn-bowling and croquet club. They're spirited and laughing and chatting and having a great time, as Lemon runs from one of her friends to the next, doing hair and nails, serving tea and cookies.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Blueberry ordered some Zippy Gro daisy seeds and planted them in her garden. She is surprised that they've sprouted in less than a day despite their descriptive moniker. Strawberry comes by to admire them and Blueberry is reminded that the new cookbook Strawberry ordered has arrived. Strawberry is excited to try out the new corn muffin recipe, as its level of difficulty will be a challenge for her. Strawberry follows the muffin recipe, mixes the ingredients, and double checks it.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While rehearsing a song alone in her cafe, Strawberry is interrupted by the arrival of Jadeybug. The poor ladybug is stressed out because her twin cousins, Kadiebug and Sadiebug, left her a message that they are coming to visit for a whole week. Jadey is unable to host them because she's having her guest room repainted.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: While at Orange's general store to pick up a package, Berrykin Bloom chats with Orange about the pogostick he gave her to help her reach the upper shelves. She is very appreciative. He ends up getting a package of special berry seeds he ordered, not the vacation magazine Orange thought he came for. He explains how the seeds were a better purchase because of all the benefits to the Berryworks but now he can no longer afford a vacation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Plum decides to hold a dance recital at Strawberry's Café Cabaret stage and sends out a stack of invitations. She forgets, however, to check the sign-up sheet at the Café and schedules it for the same night as Blueberry is scheduled to read to the baby Berrykins. Strawberry tells Plum she cannot hold the recital in the Café on her chosen day because the Café is booked.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is gathered around to watch the last snowberrykin melt. As the last bit of snow forms a puddle, a cheer goes up, spring is here! It's now time for the annual BerryFest. But with Princess Berrykin out of town on business, who will be the BerryFest Princess and preside over the festival?! Everyone is abuzz with how to go about choosing the Princess and what her duties will be. Postmaster Bee declares the only fair way to go about it is to hold an election. Everyone agrees.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake's Berry Bitty Adventures
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Friends from all over the world descend on Strawberryland to take part in the first Great Friendship Festival. Strawberry thinks the festival will provide a wonderful opportunity to see the world through different eyes.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Zoboo tells Chris and Martin he thinks he saw a dinosaur on his way to Animal Junction.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: From kangaroos to frogs, the jumpers of the wild are given free rein at Animal Junction.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Animal Junction goes buggy, and the gang realizes they are nature’s little superstars.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Zoboo is going to help Chris and Martin take care of a human baby!

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: It’s the snowstorm of the century and the gang tries to warm up a visiting macaque.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Zoboo thinks that if he had armor, like African crested porcupines, he would be safe.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: In the creature world, different smells mean different things.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Things get messy when three muddy dogs descend upon Animal Junction.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Some chicks hatch at Animal Junction, but what’s inside a strange egg they find?

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Zoboo and the Kratt brothers discover the difference between hot and cold climates.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Zoboo and the Kratt brothers find a baby hedgehog who needs a family.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Brainiac and Lost and Found are visiting and they are trying to figure out what to do.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: There are a lot of creatures that have different features that help them climb.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: Animal Junction has some super-clawed visitors.

programme.title: Zoboomafoo
programme.desc: A flying squirrel glides into Animal Junction in search of a hole to live in.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop is having a hard time finding a story she hasn't heard before. Barney and the dinos decide to tell her an old story with a new twist...by singing Little Red Riding Hood! A talented young artist is painting a portrait of Barney, but when she steps away, BJ takes credit for it. When the others beg him to teach them to paint, BJ admits the truth and learns that honesty is the best policy!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop sets up a lemonade stand, but BJ wants to fly his kite in the same spot, leading to a heated debate over sun vs. wind. Barney shares the story of The Wind and the Sun, teaching them that kindness always prevails. Rachel carelessly litters in the park. Later on, she has a dream about animals who talk. With Barney and the animals help, Rachel learns that the park is home to many. Rachel realizes the importance of nature and of keeping our earth clean!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After Riff makes friends with new kid Noah, BJ becomes jealous and thinks he is not Riff's friend anymore. Barney teaches him that you can always keep your old friendships and make new ones too. Barney and the kids are excited that they are going to the movies. But Ryan's Grandpa is coming to town, and he wants to spend some time with him. Arriving at the park, Ryan's Grandpa is looking for a very rare bird and he asks everyone to help him find it.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop wants Barney, BJ, and the kids to play with her instead of working in their garden. They promise to play with her as soon as they finish. Baby Bop can't wait, and tries many ways to get them to come play with her now, none of which work. When her friends do come to play, they have a tea party and enjoy some of the fresh things from the garden. When Riff hears a mysterious sound in the park, he plays detective, trying to solve the mystery. Barney and the children join in the fun. Along the way, they explore the sense of sound while identifying sounds of nature and the neighborhood.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After BJ loses everyone's tickets to the circus, he sets out to find them and learns a valuable lesson about responsibility. Baby Bop discovers a lonely little flower struggling to grow in a shady spot. She takes it to a sunny spot, nurtures it, and is thrilled when the flower blooms. When the flower dies on a cold day, Barney and friends help her gain hope for the future.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Three children try to build a pretend farm, but have a hard time working together. Barney takes them to a ranch to show them how all the people there have to work together to keep things running smoothly. Riff invents a Happiness Machine to try to cheer up a sad little girl. But it's her friends, not the machine, that make her happy!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ wants to learn Terry's fancy basketball moves. He tries hard, but just can't seem to do it! More importantly, he's not even having fun anymore. After some advice from Barney and Terry, BJ has fun while doing his best! On a campout, Baby Bop sees a falling star and wants to return it to the sky. With a little imagination and help from Barney and friends, she replaces the star that fell.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Everyone loves Barney! When Ryan tries to be just like Barney, he has a hard time filling Barney's shoes. Ryan soon learns that he is special when he's just being himself! Baby Bop is having one of those days! Every time she turns around, someone is telling her no! As her frustration grows, Barney explains that the word no can help you avoid making mistakes or getting hurt.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: There's a fishing contest in the park! When Ryan is tempted not to follow the rules, Barney tells The Emperor's Contest and Ryan discovers that honesty is the best policy. When Riff meets a friend who can't hear well, Riff wonders if he would still like music. Barney tells everyone about Beethoven, a famous musician who wrote beautiful music even though he couldn't hear it.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is excited for Halloween tonight! Everyone knows what they're going to be for Halloween. BJ is going to be a pirate, Melanie is going to be a cat, David will be a superhero, and Baby Bop is going to be a ballerina princess. Everyone has decided on a costume all except for Riff. Barney tries to give Riff ideas on a costume like a sailor, a firefighter, or a dancer. Riff surprises everyone in the end by wearing a one-man band costume. Then, it's time for the Halloween party to begin! Barney and his friends have fun Trick-or-Treating in the park at night. But when Ramon loses all his Halloween candies, BJ shares his Halloween candies with him.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ feels left out when the kids have a Dog Show to show off their pets. BJ doesn't have a dog and wishes he did! A friend shares her dog with BJ so he can join in the fun. Barney's friends are having a hard time deciding what to do because everyone wants to do the same thing! Barney's Native American tale, The Chase, shows them that it's best to think for themselves and not follow the crowd.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After watching birds fly overhead, Riff begins to daydream of flying. He decides to build his very own rocket ship and fly to Mars. The flight is not exactly a success, but Barney encourages Riff to never give up on his dream. After seeing many moms playing with their kids in the park, Baby Bop is curious as to what makes somebody a mommy. With Barney's help, Baby Bop learns all about what makes mommies so special.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop's blankey seems to be getting smaller; she's convinced it's shrinking. But Barney shows her that, actually, Baby Bop is growing. When Riff takes a bite of ice cream, he realizes has a very sore tooth. Afraid of going to the dentist, Barney and his friends put on a show to help Riff see there is nothing to be afraid of - the dentist is his friend.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Marcos accidentally breaks an art show project that Melanie has made. Afraid that she will be angry, he doesn't say anything, but then feels guilty. Barney helps him see that honesty is the best policy. Meanwhile, Myra, has decided she doesn't like her name. After learning about many names and what they mean, Barney helps Myra see how special her name truly is.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Melanie is sad because she misses her friend, Marisa who is visiting relatives in India. Barney takes her and the other kids on a trip to the magic caboose to visit pretend countries like Egypt, Tahiti, and India. When they were in a pretend country of India, they saw a real elephant and in the end, Melanie gets a email from her friend in India (who has also seen a real elephant in India!). BJ wants to be a great magician, but he doesn't want to spend any time practicing. He convinces Baby Bop to help him do a trick, and makes her disappear. When BJ thought she is gone forever, he realizes the importance of practice. Luckily, Baby Bop hasn't disappeared after all!

programme.desc: When design flaws fell projects, the cost is often exacted in lives.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Dublin get its name? And what do Vikings and a black pool have to do with it? Who was Maewyn Succat, and why is he celebrated with green beer? How did the harp become a symbol of Ireland, and why do the harps on Irish coins and Guinness beer look different? What is the curious history of the Book of Kells, and how did it make its way into Trinity Colleges Longroom Library?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Who were the Habsburgs, Maria Theresa, Franz Joseph and Sisi? And how did they shape Vienna? What is the Ringstrasse, and why are there so many oversized, ornate public buildings dotting it? Where did Mozart perform as a 6-year-old prodigy? When and why did Austria shrink to a tenth of its size? What do the eagles on the rooftop of St. Stephens Cathedral symbolize? And why are Viennas coffee and pastries so legendary?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: This beautiful region of England is full of picture-perfect rolling hills, quaint stone cottages, sprawling country estates and that distinct Yorkshire dialect. Why are country estates named Castles and Abbeys? What is The Shambles, and how did it get its name? Why is one of the largest medieval Gothic cathedrals in Europe in the small town of York? How did the Quakers and chocolate shape Yorks history? Where and what is Herriots country?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant church complex, the DomQuartier, in this tiny Alpine town? And why is there an equally giant medieval fortress high above it? Who lived in Mirabell Palace? And Hellbrun Palace? Why is Getreidegassse so charming, and why are those shop signs so important? And how do you solve a problem like Maria? From the Sound of Music to Silent Night to the Sonatas of this towns beloved Mozart, its time to get curious about Salzburg.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did this UNESCO town in Mexico get such a curious name? And what do a duck and a dog have to do with it? Why are there so many grand mansions in such a small town? Why do postcards have to do with its soaring Gothic church? Why is there an art school in a former convent? And how did San Miguel de Allende become a magnet for retired American military on the G.I. Bill?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why did the Scottish kings live in Edinburgh, and why dont we have Scottish kings anymore? Why was Edinburgh Castle built high upon on Castle Rock? Whats the connection between Edinburgh and Harry Potter? And where are the real-life inspirations for Harry Potter locations? What is so royal about Edinburghs Royal Mile? Who is Arthur, and why does he get his own Seat?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a stone giant holding a flame, high in the hills overlooking the town? How did silver help shape this Mexican town? Why are its streets and alleys so winding, and what happened to the Guanajuato River? What does the towns main church have to do with the King of Spain? Why does the towns marketplace look like a Paris train station? And its theatre look like the Paris Opera House?

programme.title: Earthrise: The First Lunar Voyage
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve 1968, one of the largest audiences in television history tuned in to an extraordinary sight: a live telecast of the moon's surface as seen from Apollo 8, the first manned space flight to leave Earth's gravitational pull and orbit the moon. The historic journey captivated people around the world.As this documentary reveals, however, the mission's success was far from assured. The Apollo 8 astronauts had just four months to prepare for the risky lunar orbit, and catastrophic failure would have brought a halt to America's goal of putting a man on the moon before the end of the decade.This film recounts the flight many consider to be NASA's most daring and important. Interviews with Apollo 8 astronauts, their wives, mission control staff, and journalists take viewers inside the high-stakes space race of the late 1960s to reveal how a bold decision by NASA administrators put a struggling Apollo program back on track and allowed America to reach the moon before the Soviets.

programme.title: Women Outward Bound
programme.desc: A young girl today might have a hard time believing there was a time when girls were not supposed to do what boys were doing. To them, it's normal to compete in sports, be strong, and get gritty while enjoying the outdoors. But that wasn't the case in the 1960s, when girls were expected to be delicate, clean and demure, while aspiring to secure a husband and keep house.Yet in 1965, these stereotypes didnt stop 24 adventurous young women from setting out to paddle canoes into the wilderness and live in the woods of Minnesota without any food or shelter, and who in the process, broke barriers.WOMEN OUTWARD BOUND profiles the first group of young women to participate in the rigorous U.S. Outward Bound wilderness school in 1965 and chronicles their experiences in the wild. It also captures how one month in the woods taught them they could do more than they ever thought possible. During their experience, the young women forged a special bond, and at a reunion 47 years later, the group reminisce about the lessons they learned and the memories they made, with some surprising revelations. They didnt realize it at the time, but they were a part of changing the course of history.The documentary takes the viewer on a journey over many decades with rare archival footage, personal diary entries, family home videos, photos and aerial footage of the challenging Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. In the end, the girls proved that they could always handle the difficult challenges given by naturethey just needed to be given the chance.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is a European Christmas Market also called Weihnachtsmarkt or a Christkindlmarkt? And what do these markets have to do with the Holy Roman Empire? Why do you find Christmas Markets mostly in German-speaking countries? What is the curious history of the Advent Wreath, of Silent Night, and a creepy Christmas character named Krampus? And is a Krampus different than a Krapfen?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: You may not be able to live like a royal, but wouldnt it be nice to shop like one? In this episode, we explore the curious world of Royal Warrants, a special designation given to only the best shops and services, literally fit for a king (or queen, or prince or princess). Kind of like knighthood for a shop. From the royal dressmakers to tiara makers to the royal cheesemonger (yes, there is such a thing), we take you across London, inside the world of the royals.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Who is buried under Glasgow Cathedral and why? Who is Saint Mungo, and why is he so big? Why does Glasgow City Chambers look like a palace? How did this seaport town become a center for Scottish Enlightenment? And how did the creativity of one man, Charles Rennie Mackintosh, leave a permanent mark on this city?
